zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Clifford於1305年11月5日出生在英國諾圖波藍德時，他的父親Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford31歲，母親Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles29歲。他於1328年6月2日與Isabel de Berkeley在英國格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1344年5月20日在英國英格蘭賀福爾享年38歲，埋葬在英國維斯特莫藍德沙普。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert de Clifford于1305年11月5日出生在英国諾圖波藍德时，他的父亲Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford31岁，母亲Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles29岁。他于1328年6月2日与Isabel de Berkeley在英国格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1344年5月20日在英国英格兰賀福爾享年38岁，埋葬在英国維斯特莫藍德沙普。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Clifford was born on 5 November 1305, in Northumberland, England, his father, Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford, was 31 and his mother, Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, was 29. He married Isabel de Berkeley on 2 June 1328, in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 20 May 1344, in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 38, and was buried in Shap, Westmorland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Clifford est né le 5 novembre 1305, à Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Isabel de Berkeley le 2 juin 1328, à Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 20 mai 1344, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterré à Shap, Westmorland, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Cliffordが1305年11月5日にイギリスノースウンバーランドで生まれた時，父Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Cliffordは31歳，母Maud de Clare Baroness de Wellesは29歳であった。1328年6月2日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアバーケリーでIsabel de Berkeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1344年5月20日にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで38歳で死去し，イギリスウエストモーランドシャップにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Clifford(이)가 1305년 11월 5일 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1328년 6월 2일 영국 그루스스터실르 버켈리에서 Isabel de Berkeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1344년 5월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 웨스트모를랜드 샤프에 묻혔다.

pt.5 November 1305–20 May 1344: 5 de novembro de 1305–20 de maio de 1344
pt.Brough East Riding Yorkshire England: Brough in Skipsea, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Clifford nasceu em 5 de novembro de 1305, em Northumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel de Berkeley em 2 de junho de 1328, em Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 20 de maio de 1344, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 38 anos, e foi sepultado em Shap, Westmorland, Inglaterra.

ru.Brough East Riding Yorkshire England: броую ин скипсеа, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Clifford родился 5 ноября 1305 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel de Berkeley 2 июня 1328 г. в местности биркели, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 20 мая 1344 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 38 лет и был похоронен в местности шап, вестморланд, Англия.

Se casó con Isabel de Berkeley el 2 de junio de 1328, en Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 20 de mayo de 1344, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultado en Shap, Westmorland, Inglaterra.
